title: Command Output Projection Standard
purpose: Govern how kernel command outputs compress through substrate-derived projection rather than ad-hoc summarization, so command responses behave like reversible navigable context atoms instead of monolithic JSON.
scope: command outputs that opt in via the projection envelope flag

inherits_from.rationale: Command outputs are runtime projections, not artifact kinds. The compressed-entry-projection rule that governs AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / CODEX.md / AGENTS.override.md generalizes to command emissions: a command output that fits the budget by routing instead of authoring is the runtime equivalent of a Rosetta Stone entry-affordance row. This standard does not duplicate the root contract; it applies it to the command surface.

related_authority.cybernetic_projection_feedback_candidate: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json::axiom_candidate_cybernetic_projection_feedback

principles: Command outputs compress by projection, not by ad-hoc summarization.
Bands are command-native or kind-native, not globally forced into one vocabulary.
A compact output must preserve drilldown, evidence, currentness, omission, and validation posture.
A compact output supports next-command selection, not source replacement.
Default output shapes are stable; projection is opt-in until a command's projected envelope has lived through real use.
Packet-scoped facts should be stated once and referenced by compact handles; repeat only row-local discriminators, safety-critical identity, or copy-out affordances.
Budget trimming of matrix or per-row command packets should preserve every routeable row whenever possible and drop repeated default-zero nested diagnostics before dropping rows.
Compression bands (context, deep, tape) require routed substrate; refuse the band when routing or population is incomplete (Phase 09.45 reversal).

packet_factoring_contract.name: payload_economy_via_packet_level_semantic_factoring
packet_factoring_contract.rule: Agent-consumed command packets state packet-scoped semantic facts once, then reference them by compact handles inside rows, payload blocks, next-step rows, sources, and command budgets.
packet_scoped_fact_classes: long paths and common path prefixes
omission boilerplate shared by multiple rows
allowed_repetition: row-local discriminators such as id, band, status, and short titles
safety-critical identity needed to prevent misrouting
copy-out command affordances that must remain runnable when lifted out of the packet
peer-level required envelope fields until the envelope schema itself is revised
packet_factoring_contract.success_signal: For at least one proof command, projected_size_bytes should be lower than default_size_bytes while drilldown/evidence/currentness/omission/validation remain present.
packet_factoring_contract.matrix_packet_trim_rule: When a matrix-style command packet exceeds its requested context budget, first compact repeated packet-scoped defaults out of each row: omit zero-count diagnostics, null lifecycle slots, ambient-only pressure already summarized at packet level, and default hook status. Keep row ids, row counts, status, role, control boundary, and runnable coverage/drilldown commands so the compact packet remains routeable.
non_goals: renaming paths without an expansion table

command_status_integrity_contract.name: presentation_filters_do_not_own_command_status
command_status_integrity_contract.rule: When command output is compressed, tailed, grepped, paged, notified, or otherwise presented through a filter, the projected packet must preserve the original runner status separately from the displayed excerpt. A presentation filter is not a validation receipt unless the runner explicitly preserves the upstream command status.
command_status_integrity_contract.status_source_rule: Use the test runner, owner tool, or structured receipt exit code as authoritative. If output is piped to another process, require pipe-status preservation such as `set -o pipefail`, PIPESTATUS readback, or an owner wrapper that records the upstream exit status.
command_status_integrity_contract.classification_rule: If the excerpt says green but raw_exit_status is nonzero, classify as validation_state_misreport. If raw_exit_status is absent because only a tail, grep result, notification, or copied terminal excerpt is available, classify validation as not_run_or_unproven until the owner runner is rerun or a durable receipt is read.
allowed_excerpt_uses: triage summary after raw status is already preserved
human-readable failure tail paired with full log path
compact notification paired with structured receipt id
forbidden_patterns: treating `pytest | tail` exit status as pytest status without pipefail or PIPESTATUS proof
reporting a background notification's last line as validation green
citing a grep/head/tail excerpt as the only proof that a command passed
dropping the full log or raw status when compression trims output
command_status_integrity_contract.success_signal: A compact command-output packet can be small, but still lets a reader answer: what command ran, what process supplied the authoritative status, whether presentation filters were used, where the full output lives, and whether the validator is passed, failed, blocked, or not_run.

diff_review_context_contract.name: tiered_diff_review_before_raw_patch
diff_review_context_contract.rule: Dirty-worktree review must pass through path/risk/byte metadata before a raw global patch body enters active context. Measuring raw diff bytes by subprocess capture is allowed; emitting the raw global patch body is not the default review route.
raw_patch_admission: scoped commit preflight asks for patch proof
safety or authority review requires the patch body
forbidden_patterns: raw global git diff as first dirty-worktree review on a large mixed tree
full diff plus full-file reads for the same path without a selected review reason
persisted full diff file without a compact digest and explicit scoped reopen route
diff_review_context_contract.success_signal: A diff-review packet reports raw_diff_suppressed=true, measured raw_diff_bytes, ladder_output_bytes, path/risk summaries, recovery commands, and no raw patch body.

compression_authoring_contract.purpose: Every projected command output must declare the compressed role it is serving so diagnostics can distinguish valid evidence size from low-band contract violation.
forbidden_low_band_fields: large omission boilerplate repeated per row
compression_authoring_contract.projection_rule: A command packet that is large is not automatically wrong; it is wrong when its authored contract_expectation is contents_page or bounded_entry and the emitted packet cannot support selective drilldown under budget.

band.rule: Must be a band name declared by the command (command-native) or by the owning artifact kind (kind-native) per std_kind_atlas.json. Do not invent ungoverned band names.

summary.rule: One short stable map of selectors and counts that lets a reader decide the next command without opening the payload.

currentness.rule: Carry source mtime, generated_at, recommended_action (trust|review|repair), and reason. Stale projection must be visible, not hidden.

drilldown_command.rule: A runnable command that emits the next-higher-band envelope or the underlying source for this row.

evidence_command.rule: A runnable command that opens the source authority backing this row. May equal drilldown_command when band:card already points at evidence.

omission_receipt.rule: Peer-level (not nested in payload). Names what was intentionally dropped, why, and how to recover it.

validation_contract.rule: Names the standard, freshness probe, and known failure modes that prove the row is usable.

forbidden_patterns: compact prose that hides stale source
band names not declared by the command or owning kind
context/deep/tape rows synthesized over unpopulated routed substrate
default output shape changes that break existing parsers without an opt-in flag
drilldown_command that does not actually run
omission_receipt nested inside payload instead of at peer level
declaring projection complete when the projected packet is larger than the default because it wraps the old payload without factoring repeated semantic objects

back_compat_rule.rule: When projection would change the default emission shape of an existing command, expose projection through an opt-in flag (typically --output-band) and leave the default shape unchanged. Default shape change requires a separate decision, a release note, and a preserved compatibility path such as --full for callers that still need the former complete report.
back_compat_rule.rationale: Other tooling, scripts, hooks, and docs may parse the existing JSON. Opt-in projection lets agents adopt the envelope at their own pace without breaking downstream callers.

phase_09_45_routing_gate.rule: Compression bands (context, deep, tape, evidence beyond flag/card) over an artifact kind may only be emitted when the underlying option-surface adapter actually populates that band. If the band is declared but unpopulated, refuse honestly: emit a structured row_band_unavailable response with legal_bands, populated_bands, and next_safe_commands.
phase_09_45_routing_gate.v0_safety_rule: If the band is not actually populated and emitted by the existing adapter, refuse. This satisfies the Phase 09.45 constraint without trying to solve routing-readiness globally.

must_report: projected commands and their declared bands
whether default behavior is unchanged or projected by default
repeated semantic strings/subtrees in representative packets
projected-vs-default byte posture for proof cases
stale_signals: projected command missing required envelope fields
default-projected command without an opt-in record (back-compat regression risk)
command claims a band the option-surface adapter does not populate
projected packet remains larger than default because packet-scoped facts were copied instead of factored

validation_rules: Every projected command emission must include the envelope_contract.required_fields.
row_id must follow row_id_shape and be stable for the same selector + band.
omission_receipt must live at peer level on the emitted dict.
drilldown_command and evidence_command must be runnable shell commands (best-effort lint at audit time).
Default emission shapes for existing commands must remain unchanged unless opt-in projection is requested via --output-band or an equivalent local flag.
Unpopulated bands must be refused with row_band_unavailable rather than synthesized.
Audit must report any projected command that violates the required-field set or back-compat rule.
Duplication audit must report repeated semantic objects and projected-vs-default byte posture for representative command packets before an emitter is declared payload-economical.
Diff/review context packets must suppress raw global patch bodies by default and provide scoped patch reopen commands.

atlas_navigation_use.navigation_role: Expose command-output projection envelope health, registered-command coverage, default-shape back-compat, payload-economy posture, and generic --row safety without treating the compliance ledger as command-output authority.

compliance_scanner_contract.authority_boundary: The scanner is a read-only wrapper over existing owner audits. It does not change command output shapes, refresh generated artifacts, or promote this candidate standard to active status.
